title: In Dry Bulk Nothing Goes Straight Up
section_path: In Dry Bulk Nothing Goes Straight Up
section_path_text: In Dry Bulk Nothing Goes Straight Up
summary:
Main
The dry bulk market is currently enjoying a renaissance, with spot rates at the highest level in more than ten years and the outlook for the near future appearing increasingly bright. If it wasn't for the lagging Capesize market, the Baltic Dry Index, a measure of spot rates, would have already hit 2,000, a level seen only for a few brief days during the full length of the last decade with the most recent one being the summer of 2019. Panamax, Supramax and especially Handysize vessels are earning profits corresponding to well above average returns on investment for segments that historically have been considered the "lower return/lower risk" part of the dry bulk universe.
